question: Will Labour announce a leadership election before 1st June 2026?
textDescription:
Resolution criteria

This market resolves YES if the Labour Party  announces a leadership election (OR Kier Starmer announces his intention to resign) before 1 June 2026. Resolution will be verified through official Labour Party statements or major UK news sources (BBC, The Guardian, Sky News, etc.).
newsShortReason: Multiple Labour figures have publicly declared intentions to challenge Starmer's leadership before June 1.
newsKeyEvidence: Wes Streeting confirmed he will stand in any Labour leadership race. | Andy Burnham announced plans to request permission to stand in a by-election, paving the way for his return to the House of Commons. | Labour-supporting unions predict Starmer will not lead the party into the next election. | Calls for Starmer's resignation have been made by over 90 MPs. | Streeting is preparing to resign and announce a bid for Starmer's job. | Burnham's backers are lobbying Labour’s NEC to hold an extended leadership election to give him time to return to parliament and win a by-election.
